package repositories_test

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"
	bizmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic/models"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/testutil"
)

func TestFindByProviderID_Found(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "test-find-by-provider-id",
		ProviderEmail:  "test-fbp@example.com",
	}
	created, err := repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "testfindprovider", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	user, gotUP, err := repo.FindByProviderID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "test-find-by-provider-id")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, created.ID, user.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "testfindprovider", user.UserName)
	assert.Equal(t, "test-find-by-provider-id", gotUP.ProviderUserID)
}

func TestFindByProviderID_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	_, _, err := repo.FindByProviderID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "nonexistent-sub")
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

func TestFindByProviderEmailWithSeedID_Found(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: bizmodels.DevSeedProviderUserID,
		ProviderEmail:  "seed-admin@gmail.com",
	}
	created, err := repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "seedfinduser", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	user, gotUP, err := repo.FindByProviderEmailWithSeedID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "seed-admin@gmail.com")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, created.ID, user.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, bizmodels.DevSeedProviderUserID, gotUP.ProviderUserID)
}

func TestFindByProviderEmailWithSeedID_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	_, _, err := repo.FindByProviderEmailWithSeedID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "no-such-seed@gmail.com")
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

func TestUpdateProviderUserID_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: bizmodels.DevSeedProviderUserID,
		ProviderEmail:  "update-test@gmail.com",
	}
	created, err := repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "updateprovideruser", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	_, gotUP, err := repo.FindByProviderEmailWithSeedID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "update-test@gmail.com")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	err = repo.UpdateProviderUserID(ctx, gotUP.ID, "real-google-sub-updated")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Verify the update took effect.
	user2, gotUP2, err := repo.FindByProviderID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "real-google-sub-updated")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, created.ID, user2.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "real-google-sub-updated", gotUP2.ProviderUserID)
}

func TestUpdateProviderUserID_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	err := repo.UpdateProviderUserID(ctx, 999999999, "some-sub")
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

func TestCreateUserWithProvider_DuplicateUserName(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	repo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	ctx := context.Background()

	up1 := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "dup-user-1",
		ProviderEmail:  "dup1@example.com",
	}
	_, err := repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "dupuser", up1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	up2 := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "dup-user-2",
		ProviderEmail:  "dup2@example.com",
	}
	_, err = repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "dupuser", up2)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrConflict)
}
tool_input.oldString:
package repositories_test

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun/dialect/pgdialect"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun/driver/pgdriver"

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"
	bizmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic/models"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories"
)

// openTestDB opens a *bun.DB from the DATABASE_URL environment variable.
// Tests are skipped when DATABASE_URL is not set.
func openTestDB(t *testing.T) *bun.DB {
	t.Helper()
	dsn := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
	if dsn == "" {
		t.Skip("DATABASE_URL not set; skipping integration tests")
	}
	connector := pgdriver.NewConnector(pgdriver.WithDSN(dsn))
	sqldb := sql.OpenDB(connector)
	return bun.NewDB(sqldb, pgdialect.New())
}

// cleanUsers removes test rows inserted during a test to keep the DB clean.
func cleanUsers(t *testing.T, db *bun.DB, userIDs ...int64) {
	t.Helper()
	if len(userIDs) == 0 {
		return
	}
	ctx := context.Background()
	_, err := db.NewDelete().TableExpr("\"Users\"").Where("id IN (?)", bun.In(userIDs)).Exec(ctx)

package repositories_test

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"os"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun/dialect/pgdialect"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun/driver/pgdriver"

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"
	bizmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic/models"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories"
)

// openTestDB opens a *bun.DB from the DATABASE_URL environment variable.
// Tests are skipped when DATABASE_URL is not set.
func openTestDB(t *testing.T) *bun.DB {
	t.Helper()
	dsn := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
	if dsn == "" {
		t.Skip("DATABASE_URL not set; skipping integration tests")
	}
	connector := pgdriver.NewConnector(pgdriver.WithDSN(dsn))
	sqldb := sql.OpenDB(connector)
	return bun.NewDB(sqldb, pgdialect.New())
}

// cleanUsers removes test rows inserted during a test to keep the DB clean.
func cleanUsers(t *testing.T, db *bun.DB, userIDs ...int64) {
	t.Helper()
	if len(userIDs) == 0 {
		return
	}
	ctx := context.Background()
	_, err := db.NewDelete().TableExpr("\"Users\"").Where("id IN (?)", bun.In(userIDs)).Exec(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		t.Logf("cleanup warning: failed to delete test users %v: %v", userIDs, err)
	}
}

func TestFindByProviderID_Found(t *testing.T) {
	db := openTestDB(t)
	defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck

		ProviderUserID: "test-find-by-provider-id",
		ProviderEmail:  "test-fbp@example.com",
	}
	created, err := repo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "testfindprovider", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	defer cleanUsers(t, db, created.ID)

	user, gotUP, err := repo.FindByProviderID(ctx, bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, "test-find-by-provider-id")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, created.ID, user.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "testfindprovider", user.UserName)
	assert.Equal(t, "test-find-by-provider-id", gotUP.ProviderUserID)
}

package repositories_test

import (
	"context"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun"

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"
	bizmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic/models"
	pgmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/models"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/testutil"
)

// insertTestURL inserts a ShortenedUrl row directly via Bun for test setup.
func insertTestURL(t *testing.T, db *bun.DB, userID int64, shortcode, longURL string) int64 {
	t.Helper()
	ctx := context.Background()
	now := time.Now()
	row := &pgmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		UserID:    userID,
		Shortcode: shortcode,
		LongUrl:   longURL,
		CreatedAt: now,
		UpdatedAt: now,
	}
	_, err := db.NewInsert().Model(row).Returning("id").Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err, "insertTestURL: %s", shortcode)
	return row.ID
}

func TestFindByUserID_ReturnsPaginatedURLs(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "url-list-test-sub",
		ProviderEmail:  "url-list@example.com",
	}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "urllstuser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "aaa111", "https://a.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "bbb222", "https://b.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "ccc333", "https://c.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	urls, total, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, total)
	assert.Len(t, urls, 3)
}

func TestFindByUserID_EmptyList(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "url-empty-test-sub",
		ProviderEmail:  "url-empty@example.com",
	}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "urlemptyuser", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	urls, total, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, total)
	assert.Empty(t, urls)
}

func TestFindByUserID_PaginationOffset(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "url-page-test-sub",
		ProviderEmail:  "url-page@example.com",
	}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "urlpageuser", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	for _, sc := range []string{"pg0001", "pg0002", "pg0003", "pg0004", "pg0005"} {
		insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, sc, "https://paginate.com/"+sc)
		// Small sleep so created_at timestamps differ and ordering is deterministic.
		time.Sleep(2 * time.Millisecond)
	}

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)

	// Page 1 with page size 2 should return 2 records; total is 5.
	page1, total, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 1, 2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 5, total)
	assert.Len(t, page1, 2)

	// Page 3 with page size 2 should return the 1 remaining record.
	page3, total2, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 3, 2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 5, total2)
	assert.Len(t, page3, 1)
}

func TestFindByUserID_IsolatedToOwner(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)

	up1 := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "iso-sub-1", ProviderEmail: "iso1@example.com"}
	user1, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "isoluser1", up1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	up2 := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "iso-sub-2", ProviderEmail: "iso2@example.com"}
	user2, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "isoluser2", up2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	insertTestURL(t, db, user1.ID, "u1abc1", "https://user1.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user2.ID, "u2xyz1", "https://user2.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)

	urls1, total1, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user1.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, total1)
	assert.Len(t, urls1, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, user1.ID, urls1[0].UserID)

	urls2, total2, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user2.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, total2)
	assert.Len(t, urls2, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, user2.ID, urls2[0].UserID)
}

// ── Create ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

func TestCreate_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "create-sub-1", ProviderEmail: "create1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "createuser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		UserID:    user.ID,
		Shortcode: "cr0001",

	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "taken2", "https://taken.com")
	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "other2", "https://other.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)

	require.NoError(t, err)

func TestUpdate_VersionMismatch_ReturnsVersionConflict(t *testing.T) {

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "upd-occ-1", ProviderEmail: "updocc1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "updoccuser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "occ001", "https://occ-test.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	// Use a stale (non-matching) LastUpdated to simulate a version conflict.
	staleVersion := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		ID:          id,
		UserID:      user.ID,
		Shortcode:   "occ001",
		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: staleVersion,
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)

func TestUpdate_DeletedBeforeUpdate_ReturnsNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "upd-del-1", ProviderEmail: "upddel1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "upddeluser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "del001", "https://del-test.com")

	// Delete the URL directly so it no longer exists.
	_, err = db.NewDelete().TableExpr("shortened_urls").Where("id = ?", id).Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		ID:          id,
		UserID:      user.ID,
		Shortcode:   "del001",
		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: time.Now(),
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"

	pgmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/models"

)

// insertTestURL inserts a ShortenedUrl row directly via Bun for test setup.
func insertTestURL(t *testing.T, db *bun.DB, userID int64, shortcode, longURL string) int64 {
	t.Helper()
	ctx := context.Background()
	now := time.Now()
	row := &pgmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		UserID:    userID,
		Shortcode: shortcode,
		LongUrl:   longURL,
		CreatedAt: now,
		UpdatedAt: now,
	}
	_, err := db.NewInsert().Model(row).Returning("id").Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err, "insertTestURL: %s", shortcode)
	return row.ID
}

func TestFindByUserID_ReturnsPaginatedURLs(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)

	}

	require.NoError(t, err)

	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "aaa111", "https://a.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "bbb222", "https://b.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "ccc333", "https://c.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	urls, total, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 1, 20)

	assert.Equal(t, 3, total)

}

func TestFindByUserID_PaginationOffset(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{
		Provider:       bizmodels.ProviderGoogle,
		ProviderUserID: "url-page-test-sub",
		ProviderEmail:  "url-page@example.com",
	}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "urlpageuser", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	for _, sc := range []string{"pg0001", "pg0002", "pg0003", "pg0004", "pg0005"} {
		insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, sc, "https://paginate.com/"+sc)
		// Small sleep so created_at timestamps differ and ordering is deterministic.
		time.Sleep(2 * time.Millisecond)
	}

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)

	// Page 1 with page size 2 should return 2 records; total is 5.
	page1, total, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 1, 2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 5, total)
	assert.Len(t, page1, 2)

	// Page 3 with page size 2 should return the 1 remaining record.
	page3, total2, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user.ID, 3, 2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 5, total2)
	assert.Len(t, page3, 1)
}

	require.NoError(t, err)

	insertTestURL(t, db, user1.ID, "u1abc1", "https://user1.com")
	insertTestURL(t, db, user2.ID, "u2xyz1", "https://user2.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)

	urls1, total1, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user1.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, total1)
	assert.Len(t, urls1, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, user1.ID, urls1[0].UserID)

	urls2, total2, err := urlRepo.FindByUserID(ctx, user2.ID, 1, 20)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, total2)
	assert.Len(t, urls2, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, user2.ID, urls2[0].UserID)
}

	require.NoError(t, err)

	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "occ001", "https://occ-test.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	// Use a stale (non-matching) LastUpdated to simulate a version conflict.
	staleVersion := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		ID:          id,
		UserID:      user.ID,
		Shortcode:   "occ001",
		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: staleVersion,
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrVersionConflict)
}

func TestUpdate_DeletedBeforeUpdate_ReturnsNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "upd-del-1", ProviderEmail: "upddel1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "upddeluser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "del001", "https://del-test.com")

	// Delete the URL directly so it no longer exists.
	_, err = db.NewDelete().TableExpr("shortened_urls").Where("id = ?", id).Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		ID:          id,
		UserID:      user.ID,
		Shortcode:   "del001",
		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: time.Now(),
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

package repositories_test

import (
	"context"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"github.com/uptrace/bun"

	businesslogic "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic"
	bizmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/business-logic/models"
	pgmodels "github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/models"
	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories"
)

// insertTestURL inserts a ShortenedUrl row directly via Bun for test setup.
func insertTestURL(t *testing.T, db *bun.DB, userID int64, shortcode, longURL string) int64 {
	t.Helper()
	ctx := context.Background()
	now := time.Now()
	row := &pgmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		UserID:    userID,
		Shortcode: shortcode,
		LongUrl:   longURL,
		CreatedAt: now,
		UpdatedAt: now,
	}
	_, err := db.NewInsert().Model(row).Returning("id").Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err, "insertTestURL: %s", shortcode)
	return row.ID
}

// Package testutil provides shared helpers for backend integration tests.
package testutil

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/config"
)

// FindRepoRoot walks up from the current working directory until it finds a
// directory containing AGENTS.md, which marks the repository root.
func FindRepoRoot() (string, error) {
	dir, err := os.Getwd()
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("testutil.FindRepoRoot: getwd: %w", err)
	}

	for {
		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "AGENTS.md")); err == nil {
			return dir, nil
		}
		parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
		if parent == dir {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("testutil.FindRepoRoot: AGENTS.md not found in any parent directory")
		}
		dir = parent
	}
}

// LoadTestEnv loads dev environment variables from .env / .env.dev files in the
// repository root, mirroring what the main application does at startup.
//
// If LINKSHORTENER_ENV is not already set, it is set to "dev" so that
// config.LoadEnvFrom reads the env files. OS-level values always win over
// file values — the same precedence rule as in production.
//
// It is safe to call LoadTestEnv multiple times; subsequent calls are
// effectively no-ops because OS env vars set by the first call prevent file
// values from overwriting them.
func LoadTestEnv() error {
	if os.Getenv("LINKSHORTENER_ENV") == "" {
		if err := os.Setenv("LINKSHORTENER_ENV", string(config.EnvDev)); err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("testutil.LoadTestEnv: set LINKSHORTENER_ENV: %w", err)
		}
	}

	root, err := FindRepoRoot()
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("testutil.LoadTestEnv: %w", err)
	}

	if err := config.LoadEnvFrom(root); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("testutil.LoadTestEnv: %w", err)
	}

	return nil
}

payload.prompt:
I decided to change one of the deployment options. I do not want to use nginx in MVP, I'd like to use Caddy Server instead. Could you please amend #file:app_plan.md accordingly? Please include an appropriate example of Caddy configuration for our app. Like this:
```yourdomain.com {
    # Serve Vue SPA static files
    root * /opt/linkshortener/dist
    
    # Proxy backend routes
    reverse_proxy /r/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /api/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /auth/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /openapi.json localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /docs/* localhost:8080
    
    # Fall through to SPA for all other paths (Vue Router)
    try_files {path} /index.html
    file_server
}```
If you think any other file should be amended due to this change, please let me know.

# Plan: Add Caddy Deployment Section to app_plan.md

## Context

- The previous deployment section (which mentioned nginx) was removed from `app_plan.md` in a past conversation.
- Currently `app_plan.md` has **no** nginx references and **no** deployment section.
- Sections present: 1 (Backend), 2 (DB), 3 (Frontend), 4 (Resource Efficiency), 5 (Security), 8 (Super Admin), unnumbered testing sections, 7 (Logging), Key Considerations.
- "Key Considerations" at line 741 contains: `SSL/TLS: Configure HTTPS with Let's Encrypt for production security.`

## Task

Add a new **Section 6: Deployment Strategy (MVP)** to `app_plan.md`, replacing the formerly nginx-based approach with Caddy Server. Also update the SSL/TLS bullet in Key Considerations.

## Steps

1. Insert new **Section 6** between Section 5 (Security, ends ~line 375) and Section 8 (Super Admin, starts ~line 376).
   Content:
   - Overview: Go backend runs as a systemd service on port 8080; Caddy serves as reverse proxy + static file server on ports 80/443
   - Caddy advantages: automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt, simple Caddyfile syntax, no certbot needed, runs as a system service
   - Caddy installation snippet (Debian/Ubuntu)
   - The Caddyfile example (as provided by user, with path `/opt/linkshortener/dist`)
   - Backend deployment: copy binary to `/opt/linkshortener/api`, create systemd unit file
   - Frontend deployment: `npm run build`, copy `dist/` to `/opt/linkshortener/dist`

   - New: `SSL/TLS: Caddy automatically provisions and renews HTTPS certificates via Let's Encrypt — no manual certbot setup required.`

## Files to Modify

- `app_plan.md` — insert Section 6, update Key Considerations

## Other Files

- No other files in the repo reference nginx or require changes.
- `README.md` and `Makefile` have no nginx references.
- `ai-instructions/` files do not discuss deployment or nginx.

## Verification

- Confirm no nginx mentions remain anywhere in `app_plan.md`
- Confirm new Section 6 is logically placed and Caddyfile example matches user's provided snippet
- Confirm Key Considerations SSL/TLS item is updated

## 8. Super Admin
replacements.newString:
- **User Input:** Trim whitespace, validate UTF-8 encoding

## 6. Deployment Strategy (MVP)

Caddy is the single external entry point, listening on ports 80 and 443. It handles TLS automatically via Let's Encrypt (no manual certificate management required), reverse-proxies all backend API routes to the Go process on `localhost:8080`, and serves the compiled Vue SPA static files directly. No other service or port is exposed to the public internet.

### Caddyfile

```caddy
yourdomain.com {
    # Serve Vue SPA static files
    root * /opt/linkshortener/dist

    # Proxy backend routes
    reverse_proxy /r/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /api/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /auth/* localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /openapi.json localhost:8080
    reverse_proxy /docs/* localhost:8080

    # Fall through to SPA for all other paths (Vue Router)
    try_files {path} /index.html
    file_server
}
```

> **Note:** Exact deployment tooling (Docker, systemd, etc.) and directory layout are to be decided later. The Caddyfile above shows the routing strategy; paths like `/opt/linkshortener/dist` are illustrative.

## 8. Super Admin

tool_input.file_text:
# Link Shortener — Redirect Endpoint Exploration

## Summary
Explored the entire backend codebase to understand patterns for implementing a `/r/{shortcode}` redirect endpoint. The infrastructure, repositories, handlers, and web layer are well-established with clear separation of concerns.

## Key Findings

### 1. Architecture Pattern
- **Business Logic Layer** (`business-logic/`): Pure domain models, handlers, and interfaces
- **Infrastructure Layer** (`infrastructure/pg/`): Bun models, repository implementations, migrations, mappings
- **Web Layer** (`web/`): Huma operations, viewmodels, mappers, error translation
- Framework: Gin + Huma + Bun ORM + PostgreSQL

### 2. Existing URL Management
- **UrlRepository Interface**: FindByID, FindByUserID, Create, Update, Delete (all take context + business models)
- **ShortenedUrl Business Model**: ID, UserID, Shortcode, LongUrl, ExpiresAt, LastUpdated
- **DB Model**: Same fields plus CreatedAt, UpdatedAt
- **Database Schema**: ShortenedUrls table has UNIQUE index on shortcode, FK to Users, nullable expires_at

### 3. URL Lookup Pattern
Repository already has `FindByID(ctx, id)` → needs a `FindByShortcode(ctx, shortcode)` method.
All DB queries explicitly specify columns (never SELECT *), use Bun query builder, wrap errors with context.

### 4. Current Web Routes
- GET /user/urls (paginated list of authenticated user's URLs) 
- POST /user/urls (create new URL, returns short_url: "baseUrl/r/shortcode")
- PATCH /user/urls/{id} (update URL, with OCC conflict detection)
- DELETE /user/urls/{id} (delete URL, with OCC conflict detection)

All routes:
- Use Huma registration with OperationID, Method, Path, Summary
- Extract JWT claims via GetJWTClaimsFromContext(ctx)
- Map business errors to HTTP errors via MapError()
- Use viewmodel ↔ business model mappers

### 5. Error Handling
**Sentinel Errors**: ErrNotFound, ErrConflict, ErrValidation, ErrExpired, ErrUnauthorized, ErrVersionConflict
**Error Mapping**:
- ErrNotFound → 404
- ErrExpired → 410 Gone ✓ (ready for redirect handler)
- ErrConflict → 409
- ErrVersionConflict → 409 
- ErrValidation → 400
- ErrUnauthorized → 403

### 6. Testing Patterns (95% coverage requirement)
**Handlers** (url_handler_test.go):
- Arrange/Act/Assert pattern
- Mock repositories with gomock
- Test success, validation failure, collision, authorization, version conflicts

**Web Routes** (urls_test.go):
- Build test Huma router with JWT middleware
- Test with/without auth tokens
- Verify HTTP status codes, response bodies, pagination
- Test error mappings

**Mappers** (url_test.go):
- Test conversion of business models ↔ viewmodels
- Test ShortUrl construction: baseUrl + "/r/" + shortcode

### 7. Middleware Context
- JWT claims stored in both gin.Context and stdlib context (for Huma)
- GetJWTClaimsFromContext(ctx) retrieves claims from stdlib context
- DefaultPublicPaths includes "/r/" (comment says "Public redirect endpoint (future)")

### 8. Click Tracking State
**Not yet implemented**, but instructions specify:
- Click records must go to UrlClicks table (has UrlID, ClickedAt, IPAddress, UserAgent, Referer)
- Must NOT be written synchronously (batch buffer required)
- Batch env vars: CLICK_BATCH_SIZE (default 1000), CLICK_BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 5)
- Need ClickWriter interface in business-logic/interfaces/
- Need concrete impl in infrastructure/pg/repositories/

### 9. Validation Rules In Place
- Long URL validation: https/http only, not localhost/private IPs, max 2048 chars
- Shortcode validation: alphanumeric + hyphens, exactly 6 chars, reserved word blocklist
- All validation in business-logic (ValidateLongUrl, ValidateCustomShortcode helper functions)
- Optimistic concurrency control via LastUpdated field

### 10. Configuration
All from environment variables (no .env hardcoding):
- APP_BASE_URL: used to construct short_url in responses
- MAX_URL_LENGTH, MIN/MAX_SHORTCODE_LENGTH, MAX_SHORTCODE_RETRIES
- DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, DB_* connection pool settings
- CLICK_BATCH_SIZE, CLICK_BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS

payload.tool_response:
---

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Summary

I've thoroughly explored the linkshortener backend codebase. Here's the complete breakdown:

---

## 1. **Instruction Files Summary**

### [ai-instructions/backend-business.md](ai-instructions/backend-business.md)
- **Handlers** must be pure Go with NO HTTP/ORM dependencies
- Constructor injection required: handlers receive interfaces, never concrete implementations  
- Return **sentinel errors** from `business-logic/errors.go`: `ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrValidation`, `ErrExpired`, `ErrUnauthorized`, `ErrVersionConflict`
- **Validation** (URL format, shortcode rules, SSRF prevention, expiration) lives in handlers or business logic helpers
- **Logging** required: user_id, entity IDs, error context — never JWT tokens or secrets
- **95% code coverage** required on all business-logic packages

### [ai-instructions/backend-web.md](ai-instructions/backend-web.md)
- **Web layer must contain ZERO business logic** — only request/response mapping and error translation
- **Huma registration** required; NO raw Gin routes for API endpoints (except OAuth callback & redirects)
- **Error mapping** centralized in `MapError()` function
- **Pagination** on all list endpoints with `page` and `page_size` query params
- **95% code coverage** required, but only test request/response mapping and error translation — not business rules

### [ai-instructions/backend-dblayer.md](ai-instructions/backend-dblayer.md)
- **Bun ORM** only; explicit column selection (never `SELECT *`)
- **Mappings** required: `ToBusinessModel()` and `ToDbModel()` conversion functions
- **Migrations** are numbered Go functions, append-only (never edit applied migrations)
- **Repository interfaces** defined in `business-logic/interfaces/`, implementations in `infrastructure/pg/repositories/`
- **Click batching** required: do NOT write UrlClicks synchronously
  - Batch size & timeout from env vars: `CLICK_BATCH_SIZE` (default 1000), `CLICK_BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` (default 5)
  - Must expose `ClickWriter` interface in business-logic/interfaces/
- **95% code coverage** required

---

## 2. **Handler & Repository Interfaces**

### [business-logic/handlers/url_handler.go](backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler.go)

```go
// UrlHandler contains business logic for managing shortened URLs
NewUrlHandler(urls UrlRepository, generator ShortcodeGenerator, 
    maxUrlLen, minShortcodeLen, maxShortcodeLen, maxShortcodeRetries int)

// Existing methods:
ListUrls(ctx, userID, page, pageSize) → ([]*ShortenedUrl, total, error)
CreateUrl(ctx, userID, longUrl, customShortcode?, expiresAt?) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)
UpdateUrl(ctx, urlID, userID, longUrl, newShortcode?, expiresAt?, lastUpdated) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)
DeleteUrl(ctx, urlID, userID, lastUpdated) → error
```

**Note**: No redirect handler exists yet. Instructions mention `ClickWriter` dependency but it's not implemented.

type UrlRepository interface {
    FindByUserID(ctx, userID, page, pageSize) → ([]*ShortenedUrl, total, error)
    FindByID(ctx, id) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)
    Create(ctx, url) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)
    Update(ctx, url) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)
    Delete(ctx, id, userID, lastUpdated) → error
}

**Missing for redirect**: `FindByShortcode(ctx, shortcode) → (*ShortenedUrl, error)`

## 3. **DB Schema (from migrations)**

### [infrastructure/pg/models/shortened_url.go](backend/infrastructure/pg/models/shortened_url.go)

```go
type ShortenedUrl struct {
    ID        int64      `bun:"id,pk,autoincrement"`
    UserID    int64      `bun:"user_id,notnull"`
    Shortcode string     `bun:"shortcode,notnull,unique"`
    LongUrl   string     `bun:"long_url,notnull"`
    ExpiresAt *time.Time `bun:"expires_at"`
    CreatedAt time.Time  `bun:"created_at,default:current_timestamp"`
    UpdatedAt time.Time  `bun:"updated_at,default:current_timestamp"`
    User      *User      `bun:"rel:belongs-to"`
}
```

**Indexes**: shortcode (unique), user_id

### [infrastructure/pg/models/url_click.go](backend/infrastructure/pg/models/url_click.go)

```go
type UrlClick struct {
    ID        int64     `bun:"id,pk,autoincrement"`
    UrlID     int64     `bun:"url_id,notnull"` // FK → ShortenedUrls
    ClickedAt time.Time `bun:"clicked_at,default:current_timestamp"`
    IPAddress string    `bun:"ip_address"`
    UserAgent string    `bun:"user_agent"`
    Referer   string    `bun:"referer"`
    ShortenedUrl *ShortenedUrl `bun:"rel:belongs-to"`
}
```

**Indexes**: url_id, clicked_at, ip_address

## 4. **Repository Implementation Patterns**

### [infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository.go](backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository.go)

**Key patterns**:
- Constructor returns interface type: `NewUrlRepository(db *bun.DB) interfaces.UrlRepository`
- Explicit column selection in every SELECT query
- Error wrapping with context: `fmt.Errorf("UrlRepository.FindByID: %w", err)`
- Unique violation detection → translate to `ErrConflict`
- OCC check: UPDATE with WHERE `updated_at = clientVersion`, check `RowsAffected()`
  - If 0 rows: check if exists (→ `ErrNotFound` or `ErrVersionConflict`)

**Example FindByUserID**:
```go
// 1. Count total matching records
total, err := r.db.NewSelect().Model((*pgmodels.ShortenedUrl)(nil)).
    Where("su.user_id = ?", userID).Count(ctx)

// 2. Query with pagination (offset = (page-1)*pageSize)
var dbs []*pgmodels.ShortenedUrl
err = r.db.NewSelect().Model(&dbs).
    Column("su.id", "su.user_id", "su.shortcode", "su.long_url", 
           "su.expires_at", "su.created_at", "su.updated_at").
    Where("su.user_id = ?", userID).
    OrderExpr("su.created_at DESC").
    Limit(pageSize).Offset(offset).Scan(ctx)

// 3. Convert to business models via mapping
return pgmappings.ShortenedUrlSliceToBusinessModel(dbs), total, nil
```

## 5. **Mappings Pattern**

### [infrastructure/pg/mappings/shortened_url.go](backend/infrastructure/pg/mappings/shortened_url.go)

```go
// DB model → business model (UpdatedAt becomes LastUpdated)
func ShortenedUrlToBusinessModel(db *pgmodels.ShortenedUrl) *bizmodels.ShortenedUrl {
    return &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
        ID:          db.ID,
        UserID:      db.UserID,
        Shortcode:   db.Shortcode,
        LongUrl:     db.LongUrl,
        ExpiresAt:   db.ExpiresAt,
        LastUpdated: db.UpdatedAt,  // Key difference
    }
}

// Business model → DB model
func ShortenedUrlToDbModel(biz *bizmodels.ShortenedUrl) *pgmodels.ShortenedUrl {
    return &pgmodels.ShortenedUrl{
        ID:        biz.ID,
        UserID:    biz.UserID,
        Shortcode: biz.Shortcode,
        LongUrl:   biz.LongUrl,
        ExpiresAt: biz.ExpiresAt,
        UpdatedAt: biz.LastUpdated,
    }
}

// Slice helper
func ShortenedUrlSliceToBusinessModel(dbs []*pgmodels.ShortenedUrl) []*bizmodels.ShortenedUrl
```

## 6. **Web Routes Pattern**

### [web/routes/urls.go](backend/web/routes/urls.go)

**Registration pattern**:
```go
func RegisterUrlRoutes(api huma.API, h *handlers.UrlHandler) {
    huma.Register(api, huma.Operation{
        OperationID: "list-user-urls",       // Unique ID for OpenAPI
        Method:      http.MethodGet,
        Path:        "/user/urls",
        Summary:     "List authenticated user's shortened URLs",
    }, func(ctx context.Context, input *viewmodels.ListUrlsInput) 
       (*viewmodels.ListUrlsResponse, error) {
        // 1. Extract JWT claims (returns nil if not authenticated)
        claims := GetJWTClaimsFromContext(ctx)
        if claims == nil {
            return nil, huma.NewError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized")
        }
        
        // 2. Validate/resolve pagination
        pageSize := resolvePageSize(input.PageSize)
        
        // 3. Call business handler (pure domain logic)
        urls, total, err := h.ListUrls(ctx, claims.UserID, input.Page, pageSize)
        if err != nil {
            return nil, MapError(err)  // Translate to HTTP error
        }
        
        // 4. Map result to response viewmodel
        return &viewmodels.ListUrlsResponse{
            Body: webmappers.ListUrlsToResponse(urls, total, input.Page, pageSize),
        }, nil
    })
}
```

**All routes**:
- GET /user/urls (list, paginated)
- POST /user/urls (create, returns 201 with ShortUrl constructed as `baseUrl + "/r/" + shortcode`)
- PATCH /user/urls/{id} (update with OCC)
- DELETE /user/urls/{id} (delete with OCC)

**Public paths** (in [web/routes/middleware.go](backend/web/routes/middleware.go)):
```go
var DefaultPublicPaths = []string{
    "/auth/",        // OAuth2
    "/docs",         // Swagger
    "/openapi.json", // OpenAPI spec
    "/r/",           // Public redirect endpoint (future) ← NOT YET IMPLEMENTED
}
```

func ListUrlsToResponse(urls []*bizmodels.ShortenedUrl, total, page, pageSize int) 
    *viewmodels.ListUrlsResponseBody

## 8. **Error Handling Pattern**

### [web/routes/errors.go](backend/web/routes/errors.go)

```go
func MapError(err error) error {
    switch {
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound):
        return huma.Error404NotFound("resource not found")
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrConflict):
        return huma.Error409Conflict("resource already exists")
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrVersionConflict):
        return huma.Error409Conflict("This item was modified by another user...")
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrValidation):
        return huma.Error400BadRequest(err.Error())
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrExpired):
        return huma.Error410Gone("resource has expired")  // ← Ready for redirect
    case errors.Is(err, businesslogic.ErrUnauthorized):
        return huma.Error403Forbidden("access denied")
    default:
        slog.Error("unhandled business error", "error", err)
        return huma.Error500InternalServerError("internal server error")
    }
}
```

**Available sentinels** (from [business-logic/errors.go](backend/business-logic/errors.go)):
- `ErrNotFound` → 404
- `ErrConflict` → 409
- `ErrValidation` → 400
- `ErrExpired` → 410 Gone ✓ Perfect for expired URLs
- `ErrUnauthorized` → 403
- `ErrVersionConflict` → 409
- `ErrNewUser` (for auth)
- `ErrNotImplemented` → 501

## 9. **Testing Patterns**

### Handler Tests ([url_handler_test.go](backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler_test.go))

**Setup helper**:
```go
func newUrlHandler(t *testing.T) (*handlers.UrlHandler, *mocks.MockUrlRepository, 
    *mocks.MockShortcodeGenerator) {
    ctrl := gomock.NewController(t)
    repo := mocks.NewMockUrlRepository(ctrl)
    gen := mocks.NewMockShortcodeGenerator(ctrl)
    return handlers.NewUrlHandler(repo, gen, 2048, 6, 6, 10), repo, gen
}
```

**Test example** (Arrange/Act/Assert):
```go
func TestCreateUrl_AutoShortcode_Success(t *testing.T) {
    h, repo, gen := newUrlHandler(t)
    ctx := context.Background()
    now := time.Now()
    
    // Arrange
    want := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{...}
    gen.EXPECT().GenerateShortcode().Return("abc123", nil)
    repo.EXPECT().Create(ctx, gomock.Any()).Return(want, nil)
    
    // Act
    got, err := h.CreateUrl(ctx, 1, "https://example.com", nil, nil)
    
    // Assert
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, want, got)
}
```

**Coverage include**: Success, validation errors, collisions, authorization, version conflicts, repo errors

### Web Route Tests ([urls_test.go](backend/web/routes/urls_test.go))

**Test router setup**:
```go
func newUrlTestRouter(h *handlers.UrlHandler, bl *routes.TokenBlacklist) *gin.Engine {
    gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
    router := gin.New()
    router.Use(routes.RequireJWTGlobal(bl, []string{"/docs", "/openapi.json", "/r/"}))
    api := humagin.New(router, huma.DefaultConfig("Test API", "0.0.1"))
    routes.RegisterUrlRoutes(api, h)
    return router
}
```

**Test patterns**: NoAuth → 401, ValidJWT → success, error status code mapping

### Mapper Tests ([url_test.go](backend/web/mappers/url_test.go))

Test viewmodel ↔ business model conversions, ShortUrl construction

## 10. **Main Application Wiring**

### [main.go](backend/main.go)

```go
// 1. Initialize DB connection
db, err := pg.Open()

// 2. Run migrations (dev only)
if config.GetAppEnv() == config.EnvDev {
    pg.RunMigrations(context.Background(), db)
    seed.RunIfEmpty(context.Background(), db)  // Create test data
}

// 3. Create Gin router with Huma adapter
router := gin.Default()
api := humagin.New(router, huma.DefaultConfig(...))

// 4. Wire dependencies (handlers receive interfaces, never concrete)
urlRepo := pgrepositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
shortcodeGen := businesslogic.NewShortcodeGenerator(maxShortcodeLen)
urlHandler := handlers.NewUrlHandler(urlRepo, shortcodeGen, ...)

// 5. Register routes
router.Use(routes.CORSMiddleware())
router.Use(routes.RequireJWTGlobal(blacklist, routes.DefaultPublicPaths))
routes.RegisterUrlRoutes(api, urlHandler)

// 6. Start server
http.ListenAndServe(":"+port, router)
```

tool_input.file_text:
# Plan: Implement /r/{shortcode} Redirect Endpoint

## Overview
Add a public `GET /r/{shortcode}` endpoint that looks up a shortcode in the DB, checks expiry, records a click (ip/ua/referer), and issues a 302 redirect. Click tracking is deferred for a future phase. The route is a bare Gin route (for 302) **plus** a Huma operation for OpenAPI docs. All layers follow existing project patterns.

## Decisions
- Click batching infrastructure is **deferred** — just the redirect endpoint now
- Bare Gin route for the actual redirect (non-JSON 302 response)
- Also register a Huma operation for OpenAPI docs (will document the endpoint; response can be empty 302 body)
- Capture all three click fields: ip_address, user_agent, referer — but click recording is stubbed/omitted for now
- `ClickWriter` interface not added yet (deferred)

---

## Phase 1 — Business Logic Layer

### Step 1: Add `FindByShortcode` to `UrlRepository` interface
- File: `backend/business-logic/interfaces/url_repository.go`
- Add method: `FindByShortcode(ctx context.Context, shortcode string) (*bizmodels.ShortenedUrl, error)`
- Returns `ErrNotFound` if no match

### Step 2: Regenerate `MockUrlRepository` mock
- File: `backend/business-logic/interfaces/mocks/mock_url_repository.go`
- Add `FindByShortcode` mock method following the same pattern as existing mocks (call `m.ctrl.Call`, extract return vals)
- This is a hand-extended mock (no mockgen tool available in agent mode), following the existing mock code style

### Step 3: Add `RedirectHandler` to `url_handler.go` OR create a `redirect_handler.go`
- Decision: Add a new method `ResolveShortcode(ctx, shortcode) (*bizmodels.ShortenedUrl, error)` on the existing `UrlHandler` struct (not a new struct — same handler, same constructor)
- Logic:
  1. Call `h.urls.FindByShortcode(ctx, shortcode)` → returns `ErrNotFound` if not found
  2. If `url.ExpiresAt != nil && url.ExpiresAt.Before(time.Now())`: log INFO expiry event, return `ErrExpired`
  3. Return the found URL
- File: `backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler.go` (add method to existing struct)

### Step 4: Write tests for `ResolveShortcode`
- File: `backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler_test.go`
- Cases: success, not found, expired, repo error
- Uses `MockUrlRepository.FindByShortcode`

---

## Phase 2 — DB Layer

### Step 5: Add `FindByShortcode` to URL repository implementation
- File: `backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository.go`
- Add method on `urlRepository`:
  ```go
  func (r *urlRepository) FindByShortcode(ctx, shortcode) (*bizmodels.ShortenedUrl, error)
  ```
- Query pattern: `SELECT su.id, su.user_id, su.shortcode, su.long_url, su.expires_at, su.created_at, su.updated_at FROM "ShortenedUrls" su WHERE su.shortcode = ?`
- Map via `pgmappings.ShortenedUrlToBusinessModel`
- Wrap errors: `fmt.Errorf("UrlRepository.FindByShortcode: %w", err)`
- Translate `isNotFound(err)` → `businesslogic.ErrNotFound`
- No click writes yet (deferred)

### Step 6: Write tests for `FindByShortcode` in repository
- File: `backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository_test.go`
- Cases: success, not found, DB error (mock DB or integration test following existing patterns)

---

## Phase 3 — Web Layer

### Step 7: Add viewmodel for redirect response (optional, for Huma doc)
- File: `backend/web/viewmodels/url.go`
- Add `RedirectInput` struct (path param `Shortcode string`):
  ```go
  type RedirectInput struct {
      Shortcode string `path:"shortcode"`
  }
  ```
- No response body struct needed for the Huma registration (output is `*struct{}` since the actual response is a 302 from Gin)

### Step 8: Register the redirect route in `web/routes/`
- File: `backend/web/routes/urls.go` or a new `backend/web/routes/redirect.go`
- Prefer new file `redirect.go` since redirect is a distinct concern with different registration pattern
- Two registrations:
  1. **Bare Gin route** on `router` (not `api`): `router.GET("/r/:shortcode", ginRedirectHandler)`
  2. **Huma operation** for OpenAPI docs only (returns `*struct{}{}`), with `DefaultStatus: http.StatusFound` and `OperationID: "redirect-shortcode"`
- The Huma operation handler calls `h.ResolveShortcode` to validate (for documentation purposes) but the actual redirect response is delivered via the Gin handler
- **OR** simpler: register only Huma, but override response with `huma.StreamResponse` or use Gin context for redirect. Re-evaluate after reading how auth does it.
- Signature: `func RegisterRedirectRoute(router *gin.Engine, api huma.API, h *handlers.UrlHandler)`
- Called in `main.go` after other route registrations

### Step 9: Log the redirect at INFO level
- In the Gin handler: `slog.InfoContext(ctx, "redirect", "shortcode", shortcode, "long_url", url.LongUrl)`

### Step 10: Write tests for the redirect route
- File: `backend/web/routes/redirect_test.go`
- Cases: success (302 + Location header), not found (404), expired (410)
- Pattern mirrors `urls_test.go` and `auth_test.go`

---

## Phase 4 — Wiring

### Step 11: Wire `RegisterRedirectRoute` in `main.go`
- File: `backend/main.go`
- Add call: `routes.RegisterRedirectRoute(router, api, urlHandler)`
- No new dependencies to inject (UrlHandler already has everything)

---

## Phase 5 — Verification

### Step 12: Run tests
```
cd backend && go test ./...
go vet ./...
```

### Step 13: Manual smoke test
- Start server with `make run` or equivalent
- `curl -v http://localhost:<PORT>/r/<valid_shortcode>` → 302 with Location header
- `curl -v http://localhost:<PORT>/r/invalid` → 404
- `curl -v http://localhost:<PORT>/r/<expired_shortcode>` → 410

---

## Relevant Files
- `backend/business-logic/interfaces/url_repository.go` — add `FindByShortcode`
- `backend/business-logic/interfaces/mocks/mock_url_repository.go` — add mock method
- `backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler.go` — add `ResolveShortcode` method
- `backend/business-logic/handlers/url_handler_test.go` — add tests
- `backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository.go` — add `FindByShortcode` impl
- `backend/infrastructure/pg/repositories/url_repository_test.go` — add tests
- `backend/web/viewmodels/url.go` — add `RedirectInput`
- `backend/web/routes/redirect.go` (new file) — bare Gin route + Huma doc registration
- `backend/web/routes/redirect_test.go` (new file) — route tests
- `backend/main.go` — wire `RegisterRedirectRoute`

## Out of Scope (Deferred)
- ClickWriter interface
- In-memory click batch flusher
- UrlClick DB writes
- Monthly quota enforcement

func TestDeleteUrl_RepoError_Wraps(t *testing.T) {
	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	dbErr := errors.New("db connection lost")

	repo.EXPECT().Delete(ctx, int64(3), int64(10), gomock.Any()).Return(dbErr)

	err := h.DeleteUrl(ctx, 3, 10, time.Now())
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorContains(t, err, "db connection lost")
}

// ── ResolveShortcode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

func TestResolveShortcode_Success(t *testing.T) {
	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	future := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	want := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{ID: 1, UserID: 5, Shortcode: "abc123", LongUrl: "https://example.com", ExpiresAt: &future}

	repo.EXPECT().FindByShortcode(ctx, "abc123").Return(want, nil)

	got, err := h.ResolveShortcode(ctx, "abc123")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, want, got)
}

func TestResolveShortcode_NoExpiry_Success(t *testing.T) {
	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	want := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{ID: 2, UserID: 5, Shortcode: "noexp1", LongUrl: "https://example.com"}

	repo.EXPECT().FindByShortcode(ctx, "noexp1").Return(want, nil)

	got, err := h.ResolveShortcode(ctx, "noexp1")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, want, got)
}

func TestResolveShortcode_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	repo.EXPECT().FindByShortcode(ctx, "unknwn").Return(nil, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)

	_, err := h.ResolveShortcode(ctx, "unknwn")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

func TestResolveShortcode_Expired_ReturnsExpired(t *testing.T) {
	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	past := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour)
	url := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{ID: 3, UserID: 5, Shortcode: "exprd1", LongUrl: "https://example.com", ExpiresAt: &past}

	repo.EXPECT().FindByShortcode(ctx, "exprd1").Return(url, nil)

	_, err := h.ResolveShortcode(ctx, "exprd1")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrExpired)
}

	h, repo, _ := newUrlHandler(t)

	dbErr := errors.New("db connection lost")

	repo.EXPECT().FindByShortcode(ctx, "abc123").Return(nil, dbErr)

	_, err := h.ResolveShortcode(ctx, "abc123")
	require.Error(t, err)

func TestUpdate_DeletedBeforeUpdate_ReturnsNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "upd-del-1", ProviderEmail: "upddel1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "upddeluser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	id := insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "del001", "https://del-test.com")

	// Delete the URL directly so it no longer exists.
	_, err = db.NewDelete().TableExpr("\"ShortenedUrls\"").Where("id = ?", id).Exec(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	input := &bizmodels.ShortenedUrl{
		ID:          id,
		UserID:      user.ID,
		Shortcode:   "del001",
		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: time.Now(),
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

// ── FindByShortcode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

func TestFindByShortcode_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	userRepo := repositories.NewUserRepository(db)
	up := &bizmodels.UserProvider{Provider: bizmodels.ProviderGoogle, ProviderUserID: "fbs-sub-1", ProviderEmail: "fbs1@example.com"}
	user, err := userRepo.CreateUserWithProvider(ctx, "fbsuser1", up)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	insertTestURL(t, db, user.ID, "find01", "https://findme.com")

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	got, err := urlRepo.FindByShortcode(ctx, "find01")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "find01", got.Shortcode)
	assert.Equal(t, "https://findme.com", got.LongUrl)
	assert.Equal(t, user.ID, got.UserID)
}

func TestFindByShortcode_NotFound_ReturnsErrNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := testutil.OpenTestDB(t)
	ctx := context.Background()

	urlRepo := repositories.NewUrlRepository(db)
	_, err := urlRepo.FindByShortcode(ctx, "none99")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}

		LongUrl:     "https://updated.com",
		LastUpdated: time.Now(),
	}
	_, err = urlRepo.Update(ctx, input)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, businesslogic.ErrNotFound)
}
